name: Onion
industry: AI nutrition platform - deterministic food logging, meal planning, and grocery workflows

brandVoice.about: Onion is a pre-launch AI nutrition platform built around a log -> plan -> shop -> learn loop. The AI proposes food concepts, but nutrition numbers are resolved server-side from USDA-anchored and deterministic sources; the model does not calculate macros. Shipped product capabilities include text, voice, barcode, and manual logging; deterministic meal planning; a recipe vault; shopping lists; lifestyle tracking and analytics; seven-language support; coach dashboards; data export and account deletion; and a built Kroger cart integration whose production certification is not yet confirmed. Onion is currently a web app, not an installable PWA or medical device. Stripe Pro, GLP-1 mode, and photo recognition are built but dark or partial and must not be marketed as generally available.
brandVoice.audience: Primary: health-conscious mainstream users who want planning without making nutrition a second job, and serious goal trackers who value accurate, provenance-aware nutrition data. Acquisition wedge: people dissatisfied with MyFitnessPal-style paywalls or spreadsheet-like logging. Secondary: wellness integrators and online macro or physique coaches evaluating Onion as a design partner. Messaging must remain wellness-focused and must not imply medical treatment or outcomes.
dos: use Onion as the only current product name
lead with trust: the AI suggests food concepts but is not allowed to calculate or invent macros
describe nutrition data as USDA-anchored, deterministic, and provenance-aware rather than universally verified
show the log -> plan -> shop -> learn loop using capabilities that are shipped
distinguish shipped capabilities from dark, partial, branch-only, planned, or unverified capabilities
use concrete product demonstrations such as voice logging, barcode logging, deterministic meal plans, recipe vault, and shopping lists
describe privacy with provable controls: guest mode, data export, and account deletion
use process language for health-related topics and stay on the wellness side of the claims ceiling
state uncertainty explicitly when certification, production flags, pricing, traction, or market figures are not verified
write in plain, calm, judgment-free language
donts: claim every macro is verified or imply deterministic fallback data is USDA-verified
claim Onion is a native mobile app, installable PWA, or fully offline-first product
market GLP-1 mode, photo recognition, health-condition hints, Stripe Pro, or adaptive orchestration as generally available
claim Kroger production certification or a live one-tap grocery-cart experience until certification is confirmed
promise diagnosis, treatment, disease management, medication guidance, or health outcomes
invent users, retention, testimonials, search volumes, market sizes, competitor facts, or performance metrics
publish a subscription price until the pricing decision is final
claim Coach checkout or billing is available
shame, guilt, or moralize about food
samplePosts: AI can suggest the meal. It cannot invent the macros. Onion resolves nutrition data server-side from USDA-anchored sources and deterministic rules.
Log -> plan -> shop -> learn. Onion connects the nutrition tasks that usually live in separate apps, without turning food into another spreadsheet.
Log a meal by text, voice, barcode, or manual entry. Onion keeps the routine fast while the nutrition math stays outside the language model.
Your nutrition data should be yours in practice, not just in a policy. Onion supports guest use, data export, and real account deletion.
Meal plans should fit the way you actually eat. Onion combines food concepts with deterministic portioning, then turns the plan into a practical shopping list.
